title: cerastium alpinum var. glaberrimum
intro: Cerastium alpinum var. glaberrimum, also known as the alpine chickweed or glabrous alpine chickweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and glycosides. The health benefits of Cerastium alpinum var. glaberrimum include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
